overview.tagline: A charming suburban community where history meets modern living
overview.description: Closter is a picturesque borough in northeastern Bergen County known for its excellent schools, tree-lined streets, and vibrant downtown along Piermont Road. The borough has a rich history dating back to Dutch colonial settlement and today is a diverse community with a notable Asian-American population. Closter offers a balance of suburban tranquility with convenient access to shopping, dining, and a relatively easy commute to New York City.
highlights: Top-rated public schools in Bergen County
Historic downtown along Piermont Road with unique shops and restaurants
Home to the Closter Nature Center, a 135-acre preserve
Rich Dutch colonial and Revolutionary War history
Growing Korean-American community contributing to cultural vibrancy

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Closter's housing stock ranges from historic colonials to modern new construction.
realEstate.recentTrends: Closter has seen strong demand driven by its excellent schools and charming downtown. Homes sell quickly, particularly in sought-after neighborhoods near the nature center and downtown. New construction and renovation of older homes are common.
neighborhoodNotes: The downtown Piermont Road area offers walkable access to shops and restaurants
The Closter Nature Center neighborhood features wooded lots and a rural feel
Alpine Drive area has larger homes popular with families
The western section near Demarest offers more modest housing options

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup provided twice weekly by the borough's contracted hauler.
details: Collection days vary by neighborhood
Trash in sealed bags or covered containers by 6:00 AM
50 lb limit per container
Bulk item pickup by calling DPW

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Weekly recycling collection through the Bergen County Utilities Authority program.
details: Single-stream commingled recycling in blue bins
Paper and cardboard collected separately
No plastic bags, Styrofoam, or food-contaminated items
Electronics recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water provided by Veolia Water. Sewer service managed through the borough and BCUA.
details: Quarterly water billing by Veolia
Sewer charges on property tax bills
Report water emergencies to DPW or Veolia
Annual water quality reports available

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles all construction, zoning, and renovation permits.
details: Permits required for structural, electrical, plumbing, and HVAC work
Historic preservation considerations for older structures
Zoning Board of Adjustment for variance applications
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The borough manages parking in the downtown business district and at the NJ Transit commuter lot.
details: Free parking in municipal lots near downtown
NJ Transit commuter parking at Closter station
Metered parking on Piermont Road
Residential parking permits not generally required

additionalServices.title: Closter Nature Center
additionalServices.description: Borough-supported 135-acre nature preserve offering trails, programs, and environmental education.
details: Open daily from dawn to dusk
Free admission — donations welcome
Nature programs for children and adults
Volunteer opportunities available

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Noise regulations protect the borough's residential character.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM weekdays
Saturday construction 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or holidays
Leaf blower use restricted to daytime hours and seasonal dates

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Properties must be maintained in accordance with borough code.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and kept under 10 inches
Dead trees and hazardous limbs must be promptly removed
Sidewalks must be kept clear — snow removal within 24 hours
No accumulation of junk or debris on residential properties

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dogs must be licensed and controlled at all times.
keyRules: Annual dog license required from Borough Clerk
Dogs on leash at all times when off property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Rabies vaccination required for dogs and cats

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking regulated for safety and neighborhood preservation.
keyRules: Overnight street parking restricted in residential areas
No parking during snow emergencies
Time limits enforced in downtown commercial area
Commercial vehicle restrictions in residential zones

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with borough housing codes.
keyRules: Rental registration required
Certificate of Habitability required at tenant turnover
Inspections conducted before occupancy
Landlords responsible for code compliance

other.title: Tree Preservation
other.summary: Closter has a tree protection ordinance given its wooded character.
keyRules: Permit required for removal of significant trees
Replacement planting may be required
Shade Tree Commission reviews applications
Fines for unauthorized removal of protected trees
other.source: Borough of Closter Shade Tree Ordinance

publicSchools.name: Tenakill Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Highly rated middle school known for strong academics and enrichment programs

privateSchools.name: St. Mary School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school affiliated with St. Mary Parish in Closter

privateSchools.name: Alpine Country Day School
privateSchools.notes: Located nearby in Demarest, popular with Closter families

nearbyHigherEd: Columbia University (Manhattan, accessible via bus and GWB)

whyMoveHere: Charming, walkable downtown with independent shops
Closter Nature Center offers 135 acres of trails and green space
Diverse community with excellent international dining

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit bus from Closter to GWB Bus Station or Port Authority
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit Route 9A provides direct bus service to the George Washington Bridge Bus Station. Some residents drive to the Tenafly or Cresskill bus stops. Driving to the GWB takes about 20 minutes.

costOfLiving.summary: Closter's cost of living is above average for Bergen County, reflecting its excellent schools and desirable location.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Above the NJ average, particularly for housing and property taxes

pros: Excellent public schools consistently ranked highly
Cultural diversity adds vibrancy and dining variety
cons: Bus commute to NYC can be slow during peak hours
No train station in the borough

restaurants.name: Harvest Bistro & Bar
restaurants.description: Upscale New American dining featuring seasonal ingredients and an extensive wine list.

restaurants.name: Sushi Shin
restaurants.description: Authentic Japanese sushi bar offering fresh omakase and traditional dishes.

restaurants.name: Bottagra
restaurants.description: Upscale Italian restaurant known for fresh pasta, seafood, and elegant ambiance.

restaurants.name: Panera Bread / Closter Plaza Restaurants
restaurants.description: Closter Plaza offers a variety of casual dining options including Asian, Middle Eastern, and American cuisine.

parks.name: Closter Nature Center
parks.description: A 135-acre nature preserve with 6 miles of trails, ponds, diverse wildlife, and seasonal nature programs. A jewel of the community.

parks.name: Memorial Park
parks.description: Community park with playing fields, tennis courts, basketball courts, and a playground.

parks.name: Ruckman Park
parks.description: Small neighborhood park with open space, playground, and picnic areas.

events.name: Closter Fall Festival
events.description: Annual fall celebration with craft vendors, food trucks, live music, and family activities along Piermont Road.

events.name: Nature Center Spring Walk
events.description: Guided nature walks through the Closter Nature Center highlighting spring wildflowers and migrating birds.

events.name: Community Day
events.description: Borough-wide celebration with games, food, and entertainment for all ages.

attractions.name: Closter Nature Center
attractions.description: 135 acres of forests, wetlands, and meadows with trails and educational programs. One of Bergen County's finest nature preserves.

attractions.name: Closter Public Library
attractions.description: Modern community library with extensive programs for all ages, digital resources, and community meeting spaces.

attractions.name: Historic Aulenbach Blacksmith Shop
attractions.description: Preserved historic blacksmith shop representing Closter's 18th and 19th century heritage.

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the second and fourth Wednesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Closter Borough Hall, 295 Closter Dock Rd, Closter, NJ 07624
